7. Take this assessment to discover your organization’s level of preparedness for a #ZeroTrust #cybersecurity framework. Where are you on your journey? https://t.co/8QnhhK0BIB
6.  Monitor Internet-bound traffic and activity to ensure traffic & data are not headed for malicious sites and to alert IT immediately of any suspicious activity.
5.  Leverage DNS protocol as a security control point to help detect and stop #cyberattacks early.
4. Put Identity, Authentication, and Authorization in place before providing access. Weak credentials, along with reuse of usernames and passwords across apps, expand attack surfaces.
3. Enable WAF to protect corporate applications (#cybercriminals will hijack devices to attack applications thought to be safe behind firewall)
2. Eliminate the ever-growing list of holes punched in your firewall that expose your network infrastructure to the public internet. If malicious actors can’t find your network, they can’t attack it.
1. Provide users with application-only access, not network access—on a need-to-know basis, based on user identity, device posture, authentication and authorization
